seo.meta_title: Supabase Auth vs WorkOS for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Supabase Auth (4.4★) vs WorkOS (4★) for Next.js. Compare Easy vs Advanced learning curves, free tiers, and get our recommendation.
seo.og_title: Supabase Auth vs WorkOS — Which Next.js Auth Tool Wins?
seo.og_description: Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js DX. WorkOS scores 5/10. See how they compare on pricing, features, and setup time.

hero.headline: Supabase Auth vs WorkOS for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Supabase Auth and WorkOS — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Putting Supabase Auth and WorkOS through the same Next.js project reveals a clear pattern: Supabase Auth handles server components, middleware, and edge runtime better. The 7/10 score reflects real-world testing. WorkOS is still a capable option, especially if you care about Custom pricing (enterprise).
verdict_text: Supabase Auth is the better fit for Next.js App Router projects. Here's why: the 7/10 score translates to less code, fewer workarounds, and faster middleware setup. WorkOS is still worth evaluating if no free tier (enterprise sales) is a deciding factor  –  just know you'll trade some DX for the savings.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

faq.question: Should I choose Supabase Auth or WorkOS for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Easy learning curve. WorkOS scores 5/10 with a Advanced learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Supabase Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier or No free tier (enterprise sales) is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or WorkOS more affordable?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or WorkOS easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Supabase Auth and WorkOS together?
faq.answer: Using both Supabase Auth and WorkOS in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Supabase Auth or WorkOS?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.4★. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, 5/10, and 4★. On paper, Supabase Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
